data.title: Pentair Chair Transition Raises Questions On Governance And Capital Priorities
data.description:
• Pentair (NYSE:PNR) announced the retirement of long-serving Chair David A. Jones after more than two decades on the Board and eight years as Chair.
  • T. Michael Glenn, an independent director with extensive board experience, has been appointed as the new Chair.
  • The transition marks a planned change in board leadership and governance oversight at Pentair.

Pentair focuses on water treatment and fluid management solutions, an area that sits at the intersection of infrastructure, sustainability and residential demand. As water quality, conservation and reliability remain priorities for municipalities, industries and homeowners, governance decisions can influence how Pentair positions its portfolio and capital spending over time.

For you as an investor, a chair transition of this kind is worth watching for any shifts in priorities around capital allocation, risk management and board refreshment. Glenn's appointment may shape how the Board engages on topics such as acquisitions, divestitures and longer term initiatives, which can eventually affect how the market views NYSE:PNR.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Pentair by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Pentair.

Does the team leading Pentair have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

The handover from David A. Jones to T. Michael Glenn looks more like continuity than a reset, which can matter if you already own NYSE:PNR or are tracking it after the recent pullback. Jones has been on the Board since 2003 and Chair since 2018, while Glenn has been a director since 2007 and has already chaired the Compensation Committee and sat on the Governance Committee. That history means the incoming Chair is familiar with Pentair’s water-focused portfolio, capital priorities and existing board processes, rather than arriving with an unknown agenda.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Pentair Narrative

  • The appointment of a marketing and communications focused Chair lines up with the narrative’s emphasis on product development, branding and higher value segments such as filtration and industrial water projects.
  • A long-serving internal successor may slow any push for more aggressive portfolio reshaping or sharper cost actions than those already outlined in Pentair’s transformation plans.
  • The market has limited detail so far on how Glenn might adjust priorities on M&A, buybacks or balance sheet risk, and this nuance is not fully reflected in the existing narrative.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Pentair to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Concentrated board tenure and an internal successor could limit fresh external perspectives on Pentair’s exposure to cyclical residential demand and pricing risks highlighted by analysts.
  • ⚠️ Any misalignment between the new Chair and management on capital allocation or M&A could lead to slower decision making at a time when peers such as Xylem, A. O. Smith and Grundfos are active across water technologies.
  • 🎁 Glenn’s long involvement in compensation and governance may support consistent oversight of performance targets, risk controls and investor-focused incentives.
  • 🎁 His background in strategy, marketing and communications from FedEx may help Pentair sharpen its positioning across municipal, industrial and residential water markets.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors may want to track how Glenn uses the Chair role to influence board refreshment, committee focus and capital allocation, especially around M&A, buybacks and balance sheet discipline. Attention on upcoming shareholder meetings, board disclosures and management commentary can help you see whether there is any change in tone on residential exposure, pricing, or longer term water infrastructure opportunities. It is also worth comparing Pentair’s governance moves with how competitors respond to the same sector themes.

To ensure you're always in the loop on how the latest news impacts the investment narrative for Pentair, head to the community page for Pentair to never miss an update on the top community narratives.

data.title: Nvidia Crypto Mining Lawsuit Puts Past Disclosures Under Fresh Scrutiny
data.description:
• Federal court certifies a class-action lawsuit against NasdaqGS:NVDA over alleged misrepresentation of gaming GPU revenue tied to cryptocurrency mining in 2017 to 2018.
  • The certified class covers investors who bought Nvidia stock between August 2017 and November 2018.
  • The case moves closer to trial after the Supreme Court declined to hear Nvidia’s appeal, following earlier SEC enforcement action related to similar disclosures.

Nvidia, traded as NasdaqGS:NVDA, is best known for graphics processing units used in gaming, data centers and AI workloads. The new phase of this lawsuit brings fresh attention to how the company reported gaming segment demand during the 2017 to 2018 crypto mining surge. For investors, it ties historical disclosure questions to today’s focus on high profile chip suppliers and their governance practices.

As the case progresses, the court process could surface more detail about Nvidia’s historical revenue mix and internal decision making around disclosure. Investors may want to track filings, hearing dates and any settlement discussions, because outcomes could affect legal expenses, management attention and views on disclosure quality even if core operations remain unchanged.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for NVIDIA by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on NVIDIA.

Is NVIDIA's balance sheet strong enough for future acquisitions? Dive into our detailed financial health analysis.

The class-action certification keeps a past disclosure issue alive for Nvidia at a time when investors are focused on its AI infrastructure story. The suit targets how gaming revenue tied to crypto mining was reported in 2017 to 2018, so the core question is not current operations but whether earlier segment disclosures were complete and clear. Financially, the main swing factors are potential damages, legal costs, and any knock-on effect on internal controls or disclosure practices. None of that is quantified yet, but class certification raises the chance of either a trial or a settlement process, which investors will now need to track alongside Nvidia’s AI-related announcements.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The NVIDIA Narrative

  • The case underlines one of the risks already present in the AI supercycle story. Very high growth and complex end markets can put extra pressure on disclosures and governance, something analysts have been watching as Nvidia scales data center and AI revenue.
  • Class certification challenges the cleaner narrative of uninterrupted execution. It keeps a prior period disclosure issue in view just as Nvidia is expanding relationships with hyperscalers and governments that may be sensitive to legal and regulatory history.
  • The lawsuit focuses on historical crypto related gaming revenue, while the prevailing AI factory narrative mostly centers on data centers, agentic AI and automotive. This specific legal overhang is therefore not explicitly built into growth and margin assumptions in those areas.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for NVIDIA to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged a high level of non cash earnings, and prolonged legal proceedings add one more reason to watch how quickly reported profit turns into cash, especially if litigation or potential penalties draw on cash flows.
  • ⚠️ A certified class action raises headline and regulatory risk, and any adverse judgment or sizeable settlement could influence future disclosure requirements or attract further scrutiny at a time when export rules and AI regulation are already in focus.
  • 🎁 Earnings grew 65% over the past year and are forecast to grow a further 22.33% per year, so current legal costs and uncertainty are being weighed against a business that analysts still model as growing quickly from AI infrastructure demand.
  • 🎁 Shares are described as trading at what is framed as good value compared with some peers and the broader semiconductor sector. This can give investors room to factor in litigation risk without assuming that all future AI related growth is fully priced in.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch the case docket for any motions on liability, settlement talks, or damages estimates, and whether Nvidia updates risk factors or commentary on disclosure controls in future filings. It is also worth tracking if this case triggers any follow up from regulators or affects how large customers and governments talk about partnering with Nvidia. In parallel, keep an eye on whether analysts adjust earnings or cash flow forecasts to reflect potential legal costs as the case moves closer to trial.

data.title: Should You Buy Steel Dynamics, Inc. (NASDAQ:STLD) For Its Upcoming Dividend?
data.description:
Regular readers will know that we love our dividends at Simply Wall St, which is why it's exciting to see Steel Dynamics, Inc. (NASDAQ:STLD) is about to trade ex-dividend in the next 2 days. The ex-dividend date occurs one day before the record date, which is the day on which shareholders need to be on the company's books in order to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is an important date to be aware of as any purchase of the stock made on or after this date might mean a late settlement that doesn't show on the record date. This means that investors who purchase Steel Dynamics' shares on or after the 31st of March will not receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 10th of April.

The company's upcoming dividend is US$0.53 a share, following on from the last 12 months, when the company distributed a total of US$2.00 per share to shareholders. Based on the last year's worth of payments, Steel Dynamics has a trailing yield of 1.2% on the current stock price of US$170.97. Dividends are a major contributor to investment returns for long term holders, but only if the dividend continues to be paid. So we need to check whether the dividend payments are covered, and if earnings are growing.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Dividends are usually paid out of company profits, so if a company pays out more than it earned then its dividend is usually at greater risk of being cut. Steel Dynamics is paying out just 25% of its profit after tax, which is comfortably low and leaves plenty of breathing room in the case of adverse events. A useful secondary check can be to evaluate whether Steel Dynamics generated enough free cash flow to afford its dividend. Dividends consumed 58% of the company's free cash flow last year, which is within a normal range for most dividend-paying organisations.

It's encouraging to see that the dividend is covered by both profit and cash flow. This generally suggests the dividend is sustainable, as long as earnings don't drop precipitously.

View our latest analysis for Steel Dynamics

Click here to see the company's payout ratio, plus analyst estimates of its future dividends.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Stocks in companies that generate sustainable earnings growth often make the best dividend prospects, as it is easier to lift the dividend when earnings are rising. If business enters a downturn and the dividend is cut, the company could see its value fall precipitously. That's why it's comforting to see Steel Dynamics's earnings have been skyrocketing, up 26% per annum for the past five years.

The main way most investors will assess a company's dividend prospects is by checking the historical rate of dividend growth. Since the start of our data, 10 years ago, Steel Dynamics has lifted its dividend by approximately 14% a year on average. It's exciting to see that both earnings and dividends per share have grown rapidly over the past few years.

To Sum It Up

Has Steel Dynamics got what it takes to maintain its dividend payments? Earnings per share have grown at a nice rate in recent times and over the last year, Steel Dynamics paid out less than half its earnings and a bit over half its free cash flow. There's a lot to like about Steel Dynamics, and we would prioritise taking a closer look at it.

data.title: We're Hopeful That Marchex (NASDAQ:MCHX) Will Use Its Cash Wisely
data.description:
Just because a business does not make any money, does not mean that the stock will go down. For example, although Amazon.com made losses for many years after listing, if you had bought and held the shares since 1999, you would have made a fortune. But while history lauds those rare successes, those that fail are often forgotten; who remembers Pets.com?

So, the natural question for Marchex (NASDAQ:MCHX) shareholders is whether they should be concerned by its rate of cash burn. In this article, we define cash burn as its annual (negative) free cash flow, which is the amount of money a company spends each year to fund its growth. Let's start with an examination of the business' cash, relative to its cash burn.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

When Might Marchex Run Out Of Money?

A company's cash runway is the amount of time it would take to burn through its cash reserves at its current cash burn rate. As at December 2025, Marchex had cash of US$9.9m and no debt. Looking at the last year, the company burnt through US$1.7m. Therefore, from December 2025 it had 5.8 years of cash runway. While this is only one measure of its cash burn situation, it certainly gives us the impression that holders have nothing to worry about. You can see how its cash balance has changed over time in the image below.

See our latest analysis for Marchex

How Well Is Marchex Growing?

Some investors might find it troubling that Marchex is actually increasing its cash burn, which is up 12% in the last year. And we must say we find it concerning that operating revenue dropped 5.6% over the same period. In light of the data above, we're fairly sanguine about the business growth trajectory. Clearly, however, the crucial factor is whether the company will grow its business going forward. For that reason, it makes a lot of sense to take a look at our analyst forecasts for the company.

How Easily Can Marchex Raise Cash?

While Marchex seems to be in a fairly good position, it's still worth considering how easily it could raise more cash, even just to fuel faster growth. Issuing new shares, or taking on debt, are the most common ways for a listed company to raise more money for its business. One of the main advantages held by publicly listed companies is that they can sell shares to investors to raise cash and fund growth. By looking at a company's cash burn relative to its market capitalisation, we gain insight on how much shareholders would be diluted if the company needed to raise enough cash to cover another year's cash burn.

Since it has a market capitalisation of US$62m, Marchex's US$1.7m in cash burn equates to about 2.8% of its market value. That means it could easily issue a few shares to fund more growth, and might well be in a position to borrow cheaply.

Is Marchex's Cash Burn A Worry?

It may already be apparent to you that we're relatively comfortable with the way Marchex is burning through its cash. For example, we think its cash runway suggests that the company is on a good path. While its falling revenue wasn't great, the other factors mentioned in this article more than make up for weakness on that measure. After taking into account the various metrics mentioned in this report, we're pretty comfortable with how the company is spending its cash, as it seems on track to meet its needs over the medium term. An in-depth examination of risks revealed 2 warning signs for Marchex that readers should think about before committing capital to this stock.

data.title: AbbVie Dermatology Data Highlights Immunology Depth And Emerging Skin Disease Opportunities
data.description:
• AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) is presenting new clinical research across its dermatology portfolio at the 2026 American Academy of Dermatology Annual Meeting.
  • The company is showcasing data on treatment outcomes for immune mediated skin diseases, including late breaking Phase 3 results in vitiligo and alopecia areata.
  • The presentations feature durable long term outcomes, quality of life measures, and investigational results for new indications.

For investors, this update highlights how AbbVie is leaning into immune mediated dermatology as a core therapeutic area, alongside its established presence in immunology and other specialty medicines. Fresh Phase 3 data in conditions such as vitiligo and alopecia areata can matter because they speak directly to how deep the pipeline is in specific, clinically defined niches rather than broad headline categories.

These new readouts may influence how you think about AbbVie’s R&D depth, potential future label expansions, and competitive position in chronic skin diseases over time. As more details emerge from the meeting, the focus will be on how durable outcomes and quality of life data might relate to real world adoption, pricing, and the balance between existing franchises and upcoming therapies.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for AbbVie by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on AbbVie.

2 things going right for AbbVie that this headline doesn't cover.

This dermatology update gives you a clearer view of how AbbVie is trying to extend its immunology story into more focused, immune mediated skin diseases. On one side, long term data in psoriatic arthritis, atopic dermatitis and psoriasis speaks to durability, safety and quality of life, which are key points of comparison against large peers such as Johnson & Johnson, Novartis and Pfizer. On the other side, the late breaking Phase 3 data in vitiligo and alopecia areata highlights newer, less crowded markets where systemic options are limited and differentiation can rest on real world disease control and patient reported outcomes rather than just headline response rates.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The AbbVie Narrative

  • The breadth of dermatology evidence supports the existing narrative that AbbVie is leaning on immunology and late stage pipelines to offset pressure from older products.
  • The heavy focus on a few agents like risankizumab and upadacitinib also underlines the narrative risk around concentration in immunology if future competition or safety concerns emerge.
  • Systemic programs in vitiligo and alopecia areata, and the quality of life data sets, expand the story into new indications that may not be fully captured in high level discussions of Skyrizi and Rinvoq alone.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for AbbVie to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts highlight that AbbVie has 5 identified risks, including high debt and a dividend that is not fully covered by earnings, which can limit flexibility if dermatology assets underperform.
  • ⚠️ Greater reliance on a small group of immunology and dermatology drugs raises exposure to regulatory, pricing and competitive shifts in those categories.
  • 🎁 Trading at 43.4% below one fair value estimate, AbbVie screens as good value for investors who think immunology and dermatology data will support the current thesis.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow 22.78% per year, and successful dermatology launches or label expansions could help support those expectations if clinical and commercial execution holds up.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how physicians and payers respond to the new long term data, especially on quality of life and disease control, and whether that shifts prescribing patterns in crowded areas like psoriasis and atopic dermatitis. In vitiligo and alopecia areata, focus on regulatory feedback, any safety updates and how results compare with emerging options from competitors. Investor attention is also likely to stay on how much of AbbVie’s future earnings story is tied to a handful of immunology brands versus a broader mix across neuroscience, oncology and aesthetics.
